# Requirements Document

## Introduction

This document defines the requirements for a complete modern MMORPG ecosystem website inspired by Perfect World MMORPG, built with a Neo Brutalism Dark-Light visual identity. The system encompasses a public-facing website, a player dashboard, an admin panel, and supporting backend services. The visual design combines thick borders, hard shadows, oversized typography, asymmetric layouts, floating fantasy elements, and cinematic transitions to create a premium gaming ecosystem that feels like "Perfect World Reimagined for 2026."

## Glossary

- **Website**: The Next.js 16 frontend application serving public pages, player dashboard, and admin panel
- **Hero_Section**: The primary landing area of the homepage featuring animated 3D environment, cinematic typography, and call-to-action buttons
- **Neo_Brutalism_Engine**: The design system enforcing thick black borders (minimum 2px), hard offset shadows, sharp corners, oversized components, and asymmetrical layouts across all UI elements
- **Theme_System**: The dark-light mode switching mechanism managing two complete color palettes
- **Animation_System**: The combined Framer Motion, GSAP, and WebGL pipeline handling all UI transitions, particle effects, and cinematic animations
- **Three_D_Scene**: The React Three Fiber powered 3D environment rendering floating islands, magical skybox, fantasy particles, and interactive objects
- **Auth_Service**: The JWT-based authentication service handling login, registration, token rotation, 2FA, and OAuth flows
- **Player_Service**: The backend service managing character data, inventory, mail, statistics, and achievements
- **Guild_Service**: The backend service managing guild creation, membership, territory ownership, and guild activities
- **Ranking_Service**: The backend service computing and serving player rankings, guild rankings, PvP rankings, and economy rankings
- **Marketplace_Service**: The backend service managing player trading, item listings, auction system, and realtime pricing
- **Notification_Service**: The backend service handling realtime notifications via Socket.IO websocket connections
- **Analytics_Service**: The backend service collecting and processing user behavior, server metrics, and economy data
- **API_Gateway**: The NestJS entry point routing requests to appropriate microservices with rate limiting and authentication
- **Admin_Panel**: The protected administrative interface for server monitoring, player management, economy tracking, and content management
- **Player_Dashboard**: The authenticated player interface showing characters, inventory, guild activity, statistics, and realtime widgets
- **Server_Status_Widget**: The realtime component displaying server online status, player counts, ping, and maintenance information
- **Territory_War_Map**: The interactive map visualization showing guild-controlled territories with realtime ownership updates
- **Class_Showcase**: The animated character class presentation section with hover effects, role descriptions, and 3D preview support
- **News_System**: The content management and display system for patch notes, events, and featured articles
- **Launcher**: The desktop application for game download, patching, and launching

## Requirements


### Requirement 1: Neo Brutalism Design System

**User Story:** As a visitor, I want the website to have a distinctive Neo Brutalism visual identity with dark fantasy atmosphere, so that the experience feels premium, immersive, and unlike generic gaming websites.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Neo_Brutalism_Engine SHALL render all interactive components (buttons, links, form inputs, toggles, and card containers) with a minimum 2px solid border and a hard offset shadow of at least 4px in both X and Y axes
2. THE Neo_Brutalism_Engine SHALL enforce sharp corners (border-radius of 0px) on primary action buttons and card containers
3. THE Neo_Brutalism_Engine SHALL apply oversized typography using Anton or Bebas Neue for headings at a minimum size of 48px on desktop viewports (screen width 1024px and above) and a minimum size of 32px on viewports below 1024px
4. THE Neo_Brutalism_Engine SHALL use Inter or Satoshi for body text at a minimum size of 16px with line-height of at least 1.5
5. WHEN the Theme_System is set to dark mode, THE Website SHALL apply background color #0A0A0A, panel color #141414, border color #2B2B2B, primary text #FFFFFF, secondary text #B0B0B0, accent cyan #00E5FF, accent purple #9B5CFF, accent gold #FFD54A, and accent crimson #FF4D6D
6. WHEN the Theme_System is set to light mode, THE Website SHALL apply background color #F6F6F6, panel color #FFFFFF, border color #000000, primary text #111111, secondary text #555555, accent cyan #009DFF, accent purple #6C3DFF, and accent gold #E5A500
7. THE Theme_System SHALL persist the selected mode in local storage and apply it on subsequent page loads within 50ms of page render start to prevent visible theme flicker
8. IF local storage is unavailable or the persisted theme value is invalid, THEN THE Theme_System SHALL default to dark mode without displaying an error to the user
9. THE Neo_Brutalism_Engine SHALL maintain asymmetric grid layouts where no page section uses equal-width columns, ensuring at least one column per section row differs in width by a minimum of 20% from adjacent columns

### Requirement 2: Homepage Hero Section

**User Story:** As a visitor, I want to see a cinematic hero section with animated 3D fantasy environment and bold call-to-action, so that I am immediately immersed in the Perfect World atmosphere.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Hero_Section SHALL display the heading text "ENTER THE PERFECT WORLD" using Anton font at a minimum size of 72px on desktop viewports (>=1024px) and a minimum size of 36px on mobile viewports (<768px)
2. THE Three_D_Scene SHALL render a floating island environment with animated clouds, mystical sky, and cinematic particle system within the Hero_Section background
3. THE Hero_Section SHALL display a "PLAY NOW" primary action button with Neo Brutalism styling including thick border and hard shadow
4. THE Hero_Section SHALL display a "DOWNLOAD LAUNCHER" secondary action button within the same button group as the primary button
5. THE Hero_Section SHALL display a "WATCH TRAILER" text link below the action buttons
6. WHEN the homepage loads, THE Server_Status_Widget SHALL display the current number of online players within the Hero_Section, updated at least every 30 seconds via the websocket connection
7. IF the Server_Status_Widget fails to retrieve player count data, THEN THE Hero_Section SHALL display a fallback indicator showing that server status is unavailable without blocking the rendering of other Hero_Section content
8. THE Animation_System SHALL apply parallax scrolling to at least 2 Hero_Section background layers at distinct scroll speed ratios while maintaining a minimum frame rate of 30fps
9. THE Three_D_Scene SHALL render floating magical symbols and dark fantasy atmospheric effects using React Three Fiber with a target frame rate of 60fps on devices supporting WebGL 2.0
10. IF the visitor's device does not support WebGL, THEN THE Hero_Section SHALL display a static background image fallback in place of the Three_D_Scene
11. WHILE the Three_D_Scene is loading, THE Hero_Section SHALL display a loading placeholder that preserves the section layout dimensions until the 3D content is ready to render

### Requirement 3: Server Status Display

**User Story:** As a player, I want to see realtime server status information, so that I can choose the best server to play on and know if maintenance is scheduled.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Server_Status_Widget SHALL display each game server with its name, online/offline/busy status indicator, current player count, and ping latency in milliseconds
2. THE Server_Status_Widget SHALL indicate a server as "busy" WHEN its current player count reaches or exceeds 80% of the server's maximum player capacity
3. WHEN a server status changes, THE Server_Status_Widget SHALL update the display within 5 seconds without requiring a page refresh
4. WHILE a server is in maintenance mode, THE Server_Status_Widget SHALL display a maintenance indicator with estimated completion time if available, or a message indicating that the completion time is not yet determined
5. THE Server_Status_Widget SHALL display a minimum of 4 and a maximum of 20 server entries in a grid layout with Neo Brutalism card styling
6. WHEN a player hovers over a server card, THE Animation_System SHALL apply a glowing border effect and elevation change to the card
7. WHILE server data is being retrieved for the first time, THE Server_Status_Widget SHALL display a loading skeleton placeholder for each server card until data is received or a timeout of 10 seconds is reached
8. IF server status data cannot be retrieved or the retrieval timeout of 10 seconds is exceeded, THEN THE Server_Status_Widget SHALL display an error message indicating that server information is temporarily unavailable and SHALL provide a manual retry option

### Requirement 4: Class Showcase Section

**User Story:** As a prospective player, I want to browse character classes with animated previews and role descriptions, so that I can understand the gameplay options before downloading.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Class_Showcase SHALL display a minimum of 4 character class cards, each showing the class name, a role description of no more than 150 characters, and representative artwork
2. WHEN a player hovers over a class card, THE Animation_System SHALL scale the card to 1.05x its original size within 200 milliseconds, apply a glowing border effect, and reveal additional class details including primary abilities and playstyle summary
3. THE Class_Showcase SHALL categorize classes by role type (tank, healer, damage dealer, support) with a distinct icon and label for each role type
4. WHEN a player selects a class card on a device with WebGL support, THE Class_Showcase SHALL display a 3D character model preview that the player can rotate
5. IF a device does not support WebGL, THEN THE Class_Showcase SHALL display a static image fallback at a minimum resolution of 600x800 pixels for character previews
6. WHEN a player hovers out of a class card, THE Animation_System SHALL revert the card to its original size and appearance within 200 milliseconds

### Requirement 5: Territory War Map

**User Story:** As a player, I want to view an interactive territory war map showing guild-controlled regions in realtime, so that I can track territorial conflicts and guild dominance.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Territory_War_Map SHALL render a pannable and zoomable map displaying all contestable territories with color-coded guild ownership, where each controlling guild is assigned a distinct color, and uncontrolled territories are displayed in a neutral gray tone
2. WHEN a territory ownership changes, THE Territory_War_Map SHALL update the display within 10 seconds via websocket connection
3. WHEN a player clicks on a guild-controlled territory, THE Territory_War_Map SHALL display a detail panel showing the controlling guild name, capture date, and defense strength as a numeric value from 0 to 100
4. IF a player clicks on an uncontrolled territory, THEN THE Territory_War_Map SHALL display a detail panel indicating the territory is unclaimed and showing the territory name
5. THE Territory_War_Map SHALL display animated battle indicators using a pulsing border effect on territories currently under siege
6. THE Territory_War_Map SHALL apply Neo Brutalism styling with a minimum 2px solid border on territory boundaries and a hard offset shadow of at least 4px on the map container

### Requirement 6: World Rankings

**User Story:** As a competitive player, I want to view comprehensive rankings across multiple categories, so that I can track my progress and compare with other players.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Ranking_Service SHALL compute and serve rankings in four categories: Top Players (sorted by level descending, then experience points descending as tiebreaker), Top Guilds (sorted by territory count descending, then member count descending as tiebreaker), Top PvP (sorted by arena rating descending, then kill/death ratio descending as tiebreaker), and Top Economy (sorted by total currency holdings descending)
2. THE Website SHALL display ranking tables with columns: rank position, player or guild name, category-specific score (experience points for Top Players, territory count for Top Guilds, arena rating for Top PvP, currency amount for Top Economy), and guild affiliation where applicable, displaying "No Guild" for unaffiliated players
3. WHEN ranking data updates, THE Website SHALL reflect changes within 60 seconds on the public rankings page
4. THE Website SHALL display the top 10 entries per category on the homepage rankings section with a link to full rankings
5. THE Website SHALL apply Neo Brutalism table styling with minimum 2px solid row borders, alternating panel backgrounds using the Theme_System panel and background colors, and bold rank numbers
6. THE Website SHALL display a maximum of 100 entries per page on the full rankings page with pagination controls to navigate additional entries
7. IF two or more entries share the same rank score and tiebreaker value within a category, THEN THE Ranking_Service SHALL assign them the same rank position and skip subsequent positions accordingly


### Requirement 7: News and Content System

**User Story:** As a player, I want to read game news, patch notes, and event announcements, so that I stay informed about game updates and community activities.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE News_System SHALL display a featured news hero card with image at a minimum size of 600x300px, title, excerpt of maximum 200 characters, and publication date
2. THE News_System SHALL categorize articles into: Patch Notes, Events, Community, and Announcements
3. WHEN a new article is published, THE News_System SHALL display it on the homepage news section within 5 minutes
4. THE News_System SHALL display a maximum of 6 animated news cards on the homepage news section with Neo Brutalism styling including minimum 2px borders and hard offset shadows of at least 4px
5. WHEN a player hovers over a news card, THE Animation_System SHALL apply a vertical lift of 4px to 8px and a border glow transition completing within 300ms
6. WHEN a player selects a category filter, THE News_System SHALL display only articles matching the selected category and indicate the active filter state
7. WHEN a player clicks on a news card, THE News_System SHALL navigate to a full article view displaying the title, publication date, category, author, featured image, and full article body
8. IF no articles exist for a selected category, THEN THE News_System SHALL display an empty state message indicating no articles are available for that category

### Requirement 8: Marketplace System

**User Story:** As a player, I want to trade items with other players through a marketplace with realtime pricing, so that I can buy and sell game items efficiently.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Marketplace_Service SHALL support item listing creation with item name (1-100 characters), description (0-500 characters), quantity (1-9999), asking price (0.01-999,999,999.99 in-game currency), and listing duration (1-168 hours)
2. THE Marketplace_Service SHALL support auction-style listings with starting bid (minimum 0.01 in-game currency), minimum bid increment (minimum 0.01 in-game currency), and countdown timer (1-168 hours)
3. WHEN a new bid is placed on an auction, THE Marketplace_Service SHALL notify the previous highest bidder within 3 seconds via the Notification_Service
4. IF an item has at least 5 previous sales, THEN THE Marketplace_Service SHALL display price history covering the most recent 90 days, updated within 10 seconds of a completed sale
5. WHILE a player is browsing the marketplace, THE Website SHALL update item availability and pricing within 5 seconds of any change via websocket connection
6. THE Marketplace_Service SHALL enforce a transaction fee of a configurable percentage (0.1%-25%) on completed sales, deducted from the seller's proceeds
7. IF a listing or auction expires without a completed sale, THEN THE Marketplace_Service SHALL return the listed items to the seller's inventory within 10 seconds and notify the seller via the Notification_Service
8. IF a buyer has insufficient funds to complete a purchase or a bidder places a bid below the current highest bid plus the minimum increment, THEN THE Marketplace_Service SHALL reject the transaction and display an error message indicating the reason for rejection

### Requirement 9: Authentication and Security

**User Story:** As a player, I want secure account access with multiple authentication options, so that my account and game assets are protected.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Auth_Service SHALL support email/password registration with email verification requirement before account activation, where the verification link expires after 24 hours
2. THE Auth_Service SHALL support OAuth login via Discord and Google providers
3. THE Auth_Service SHALL implement JWT access tokens with a maximum lifetime of 15 minutes and refresh token rotation with a maximum lifetime of 7 days
4. THE Auth_Service SHALL support optional two-factor authentication using TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password)
5. IF a login attempt fails 5 times consecutively for the same account, THEN THE Auth_Service SHALL lock the account for 15 minutes and notify the account owner via email
6. IF CAPTCHA verification fails on registration or password recovery forms, THEN THE Auth_Service SHALL reject the submission and prompt the user to complete a new CAPTCHA challenge
7. THE API_Gateway SHALL enforce rate limiting of 100 requests per minute per authenticated user and 30 requests per minute per unauthenticated IP address
8. THE Auth_Service SHALL support password recovery via email with a time-limited reset token valid for 30 minutes
9. THE Auth_Service SHALL require passwords to contain a minimum of 8 characters, at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one digit, and one special character, with a maximum length of 128 characters
10. WHEN a user changes their password or enables two-factor authentication, THE Auth_Service SHALL invalidate all existing refresh tokens for that account

### Requirement 10: Player Dashboard

**User Story:** As an authenticated player, I want a comprehensive dashboard showing my characters, inventory, guild activity, and game statistics, so that I can manage my game experience outside the game client.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Player_Dashboard SHALL display an overview page with online status indicator, character summary cards (showing name, class, and level for each character), the 10 most recent notifications, and active event tracker
2. THE Player_Dashboard SHALL display a character management page listing all characters (up to a maximum of 10 per account) with name, class, level, and equipped gear slots summary
3. THE Player_Dashboard SHALL display an inventory page with a paginated item grid (maximum 50 items per page), item details panel on selection showing item name, type, stats, and quantity, and item transfer between the player's own characters
4. THE Player_Dashboard SHALL display guild activity feed showing the 20 most recent guild events, member online status, and territory war schedule
5. THE Player_Dashboard SHALL display statistics page with playtime charts for selectable periods (7, 30, and 90 days), combat statistics including kills, deaths, and win rate, economy history showing currency earned and spent, and achievement progress as completed count out of total
6. THE Player_Dashboard SHALL apply Neo Brutalism styling consistent with the Neo_Brutalism_Engine including floating cards with thick borders and hard shadows, animated stat counters, and glowing accent borders using the Theme_System color palette
7. WHEN a realtime event occurs (notification, guild activity, world boss spawn), THE Notification_Service SHALL push updates to the Player_Dashboard within 2 seconds via websocket
8. IF the Player_Dashboard fails to load data for any section, THEN THE Player_Dashboard SHALL display an error indicator in the affected section with a retry option while keeping other sections functional
9. IF the authenticated player has no characters, no guild membership, or an empty inventory, THEN THE Player_Dashboard SHALL display an empty state message with guidance on how to get started for each respective section
10. IF a user attempts to access the Player_Dashboard without a valid authenticated session, THEN THE Website SHALL redirect the user to the login page

### Requirement 11: Admin Panel

**User Story:** As a game administrator, I want a comprehensive management panel with realtime monitoring and player management tools, so that I can maintain server health and enforce game rules.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Admin_Panel SHALL display server metrics including CPU usage percentage, memory usage percentage, active connection count, and requests per second, updated within the interval defined by criterion 7
2. THE Admin_Panel SHALL provide player search returning a maximum of 50 results per page, with ability to view player details (username, account status, login history, currency balance, ban history), issue warnings, apply temporary bans with a specified duration between 1 hour and 365 days, and apply permanent bans
3. THE Admin_Panel SHALL display economy monitoring dashboards showing total currency in circulation, marketplace transaction volume over the last 24 hours, and inflation rate calculated as percentage change in average item price compared to the previous 7-day period
4. THE Admin_Panel SHALL provide a patch deployment interface for publishing game updates with version notes of up to 5000 characters and scheduled maintenance windows requiring a minimum of 30 minutes advance notice before activation
5. THE Admin_Panel SHALL maintain an audit log recording all administrative actions with administrator identity, action type, target, and timestamp, retained for a minimum of 90 days
6. THE Admin_Panel SHALL enforce role-based access control where moderators may only issue warnings and temporary bans, game masters may additionally apply permanent bans and view economy dashboards, and senior administrators have full access including patch deployment and role management
7. WHILE the Admin_Panel is active, THE Analytics_Service SHALL push live metrics updates via websocket connection at a maximum interval of 5 seconds
8. IF the websocket connection to the Analytics_Service is lost, THEN THE Admin_Panel SHALL display a connection status indicator showing disconnected state and attempt to reconnect automatically every 10 seconds
9. IF an administrative action (warning, ban, or patch deployment) fails, THEN THE Admin_Panel SHALL display an error message indicating the failure reason and preserve any data entered by the administrator
10. THE Admin_Panel SHALL apply dark tactical command center styling with high-density information layout, cyber fantasy aesthetic, and Neo Brutalism component patterns


### Requirement 12: Navigation and Page Structure

**User Story:** As a visitor, I want clear and accessible navigation across all public pages, so that I can find game information, community features, and support resources easily.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Website SHALL display a persistent navigation bar with links to: Home, Download, News, Rankings, Guilds, Territory War, Marketplace, Wiki, Support, Login, and Play Now, where each link is keyboard-focusable and indicates the currently active page with a visible highlight
2. THE Website SHALL display the Perfect World logo in the navigation bar as a home link
3. WHEN a visitor scrolls down the page by any distance greater than 0 pixels, THE Website SHALL keep the navigation bar fixed at the top of the viewport with a backdrop blur effect
4. THE Website SHALL display a footer with logo, quick links section (containing links to Download, News, Rankings, and Wiki), support links section (containing links to Support, FAQ, and Terms of Service), social media links, and a newsletter subscription form
5. THE Website SHALL render a "YOUR JOURNEY STARTS NOW" call-to-action section above the footer with a Create Account button and a Download Launcher button
6. WHEN the viewport width is below 768px, THE Website SHALL collapse the navigation into a hamburger menu button that, when activated, opens a slide-out panel within 300ms displaying all navigation links listed in criterion 1
7. WHEN a visitor activates the hamburger menu button while the slide-out panel is open, THE Website SHALL close the slide-out panel within 300ms
8. IF a visitor submits the newsletter subscription form with an invalid or empty email address, THEN THE Website SHALL display an inline error message indicating the email format requirement without clearing the entered value
9. WHEN a visitor submits the newsletter subscription form with a valid email address, THE Website SHALL display a confirmation message indicating successful subscription

### Requirement 13: Animation and Interaction System

**User Story:** As a visitor, I want smooth cinematic animations and interactive feedback throughout the website, so that the browsing experience feels immersive and premium.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. WHEN a section scrolls into the viewport by at least 20% of its height, THE Animation_System SHALL trigger an entrance animation on that section using Framer Motion with a default duration between 300ms and 800ms and configurable easing
2. WHEN a route change occurs, THE Animation_System SHALL apply a page transition animation with a maximum transition duration of 400ms
3. THE Animation_System SHALL render particle effects in the background of hero sections using WebGL, maintaining a particle count that does not cause frame rate to drop below the threshold defined in criterion 6
4. WHEN a user interacts with a button or card, THE Animation_System SHALL provide visual feedback within 100ms including scale, shadow, or glow changes
5. IF the user's operating system has prefers-reduced-motion enabled, THEN THE Animation_System SHALL disable all animations except page transition animations and interactive feedback responses, replacing entrance animations and particle effects with static presentation
6. WHILE any animation is active, THE Animation_System SHALL maintain a minimum frame rate of 30fps on devices with a dedicated GPU or integrated GPU released within the last 5 years

### Requirement 14: Responsive Design and Performance

**User Story:** As a player accessing the website from various devices, I want the interface to adapt to my screen size and load quickly, so that I have a good experience regardless of device.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Website SHALL render layouts supporting viewport widths from 320px to 2560px with breakpoints at 768px, 1024px, and 1440px, ensuring no horizontal scrolling, no content overflow beyond viewport bounds, and no overlapping interactive elements at any supported width
2. THE Website SHALL achieve a Lighthouse Performance score of at least 80 on desktop and 70 on mobile for the homepage
3. THE Website SHALL implement lazy loading for images, 3D scenes, and content sections not visible within the initial viewport, deferring their network requests until the user scrolls within 200px of the content
4. IF the device GPU supports only WebGL 1.0 or lacks required WebGL extensions, THEN THE Three_D_Scene SHALL reduce rendering quality by disabling post-processing effects and reducing texture resolution to maintain a minimum frame rate of 24fps
5. THE Website SHALL implement server-side rendering for all pages accessible without authentication to ensure content is indexable by search engines
6. WHEN the initial page load occurs on a 10 Mbps connection, THE Website SHALL render the primary navigation and above-the-fold text and images within 2 seconds as measured by Largest Contentful Paint
7. IF the Three_D_Scene fails to initialize due to missing WebGL support, THEN THE Website SHALL display a static fallback image representing the 3D content within 1 second of detection

### Requirement 15: Realtime Communication Infrastructure

**User Story:** As a player, I want realtime updates for server status, marketplace changes, notifications, and territory war events, so that I always see current information without manual refreshing.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Notification_Service SHALL establish a Socket.IO websocket connection for each authenticated user session within 5 seconds of the connection request
2. WHEN a websocket connection is lost, THE Website SHALL attempt automatic reconnection with exponential backoff starting at 1 second, doubling each attempt, up to a maximum interval of 30 seconds, for a maximum of 10 attempts
3. THE Notification_Service SHALL deliver messages to clients subscribed to any of the following channels: server-status, marketplace-updates, guild-events, territory-war, and personal-notifications
4. IF the websocket connection cannot be established or all 10 reconnection attempts are exhausted, THEN THE Website SHALL fall back to HTTP polling at 30-second intervals for server-status, marketplace-updates, and territory-war channels
5. THE API_Gateway SHALL authenticate websocket connections usin

<content>
*For any* game server with a playerCount and maxPlayers where both are non-negative integers and maxPlayers > 0, the server status SHALL be computed as 'busy' if and only if playerCount / maxPlayers >= 0.8.

**Validates: Requirements 3.2**

### Property 4: Server Status Display Completeness

*For any* valid server status data object containing name, status, playerCount, and ping, the rendered Server Status Widget output SHALL contain all four data fields.

**Validates: Requirements 3.1**

### Property 5: Class Description Truncation

*For any* character class with a description string of arbitrary length, the displayed description text SHALL never exceed 150 characters.

**Validates: Requirements 4.1**

### Property 6: Class Role Categorization

*For any* character class in the system, the role categorization function SHALL assign exactly one role type from the set {tank, healer, damage_dealer, support}, and every class SHALL have a role assignment.

**Validates: Requirements 4.3**

### Property 7: Territory Defense Strength Bounds

*For any* territory data object, the defense strength value displayed in the detail panel SHALL always be within the inclusive range [0, 100].

**Validates: Requirements 5.3**

### Property 8: Ranking Sort Order Correctness

*For any* list of entities within a ranking category, the ranking function SHALL produce a list sorted by primary score descending, then by tiebreaker value descending, such that for any two adjacent entries (rank i, rank i+1), entry i has a score >= entry i+1, and if scores are equal, entry i has tiebreaker >= entry i+1.

**Validates: Requirements 6.1**

### Property 9: Tied Rank Position Assignment

*For any* set of ranking entries where two or more entries share the same score AND the same tiebreaker value, those entries SHALL be assigned the same rank position, and the next distinct entry SHALL receive a rank position equal to the tied rank plus the count of tied entries (dense ranking with gaps).

**Validates: Requirements 6.7**

### Property 10: Ranking Display Field Completeness

*For any* rank entry, the rendered table row SHALL contain: rank position (integer), entity name (string), category-specific score, and guild affiliation (guild name or "No Guild" for unaffiliated players).

**Validates: Requirements 6.2**

### Property 11: Pagination Correctness

*For any* total entry count N and page size P, the pagination function SHALL produce exactly ceil(N/P) pages, each page (except possibly the last) containing exactly P entries, and the last page containing between 1 and P entries inclusive.

**Validates: Requirements 6.6**

### Property 12: News Excerpt Truncation

*For any* news article with an excerpt field of arbitrary length, the displayed excerpt SHALL never exceed 200 characters.

**Validates: Requirements 7.1**

### Property 13: Article Category Membership

*For any* news article in the system, its category SHALL be exactly one of: PATCH_NOTES, EVENTS, COMMUNITY, or ANNOUNCEMENTS.

**Validates: Requirements 7.2**

### Property 14: Category Filter Correctness

*For any* set of articles and any selected category filter, the filtered result set SHALL contain only articles whose category matches the selected filter, and SHALL contain all articles from the original set that match.

**Validates: Requirements 7.6**

### Property 15: Marketplace Input Validation

*For any* listing creation input, the validation SHALL accept the input if and only if: name length is in [1, 100], description length is in [0, 500], quantity is an integer in [1, 9999], price is in [0.01, 999999999.99], and duration is an integer in [1, 168]. For auction inputs, additionally: startingBid >= 0.01 and minIncrement >= 0.01.

**Validates: Requirements 8.1, 8.2**

### Property 16: Transaction Fee Computation

*For any* completed marketplace sale with price P and configured fee percentage F (where 0.1 <= F <= 25.0), the seller's proceeds SHALL equal P * (1 - F/100), and the fee deducted SHALL equal P * (F/100).

**Validates: Requirements 8.6**

### Property 17: Price History Visibility Threshold

*For any* item in the marketplace, price history SHALL be displayed if and only if the item has at least 5 completed sales in its history. When displayed, it SHALL cover only sales within the most recent 90 days.

**Validates: Requirements 8.4**

### Property 18: Bid and Purchase Rejection

*For any* bid on an auction where the bid amount is less than (currentBid + minIncrement), the bid SHALL be rejected. *For any* purchase attempt where the buyer's available currency is less than the listing price times quantity, the purchase SHALL be rejected.

**Validates: Requirements 8.8**

### Property 19: Password Validation

*For any* string S, the password validator SHALL accept S if and only if: length(S) >= 8 AND length(S) <= 128 AND S contains at least one uppercase letter AND S contains at least one lowercase letter AND S contains at least one digit AND S contains at least one special character (non-alphanumeric).

**Validates: Requirements 9.9**

### Property 20: Token Refresh Rotation

*For any* valid refresh token used to obtain a new token pair, the operation SHALL return a new access token (expiry 15 minutes) and a new refresh token (expiry 7 days), AND the original refresh token SHALL become invalid for subsequent use.

**Validates: Requirements 9.3**

### Property 21: Account Lock After Failed Attempts

*For any* account with N consecutive failed login attempts, the account SHALL be locked if and only if N >= 5. When locked, the lock duration SHALL be 15 minutes. Successful login SHALL reset the failed attempt counter to 0.

**Validates: Requirements 9.5**

### Property 22: Token Invalidation on Security Change

*For any* account with one or more active refresh tokens, when the user changes their password or enables 2FA, ALL existing refresh tokens for that account SHALL become invalid.

**Validates: Requirements 9.10**

### Property 23: Rate Limiting

*For any* authenticated user making N requests within a 60-second window, requests SHALL be allowed when N <= 100 and rejected when N > 100. *For any* unauthenticated IP making N requests within a 60-second window, requests SHALL be allowed when N <= 30 and rejected when N > 30.

**Validates: Requirements 9.7**

### Property 24: RBAC Permission Enforcement

*For any* combination of user role and administrative action, the permission check SHALL: allow moderators only warnings and temporary bans; allow game masters additionally permanent bans and economy dashboard access; allow senior administrators all actions including patch deployment and role management. Any action not permitted for a role SHALL be rejected.

**Validates: Requirements 11.6**

### Property 25: Exponential Backoff Delay Computation

*For any* reconnection attempt number N (where 1 <= N <= 10), the computed reconnection delay SHALL equal min(1000 * 2^(N-1), 30000) milliseconds.

**Validates: Requirements 15.2**

### Property 26: Trade Atomicity

*For any* marketplace trade operation (purchase or auction completion) that fails at any point during execution, both the buyer's and seller's inventories and currency balances SHALL remain unchanged from their pre-trade state.

**Validates: Requirements 16.5, 16.6**

### Property 27: Guild Description Truncation

*For any* guild with a description string of arbitrary length, the displayed description SHALL never exceed 500 characters.

**Validates: Requirements 18.1**

### Property 28: Guild Search Filter Correctness

*For any* guild search with active filters (minimum level, territory count, recruitment status), all returned results SHALL satisfy every active filter condition, and no guild satisfying all conditions SHALL be excluded from results. Each page SHALL contain at most 20 results.

**Validates: Requirements 18.3**

### Property 29: Guild Permission Enforcement

*For any* guild member with rank below LEADER attempting a management action (promote, demote, or remove another member), the action SHALL be rejected with a permission error.

**Validates: Requirements 18.7**

### Property 30: Support Ticket Field Validation

*For any* support ticket submission, the validation SHALL reject the submission if: subject length exceeds 150 characters, description length exceeds 2000 characters, more than 3 file attachments are included, or any single attachment exceeds 5MB. Valid submissions meeting all constraints SHALL be accepted.

**Validates: Requirements 19.1**

## Error Handling

### Error Handling Strategy

The system uses a layered error handling approach:

| Layer | Strategy | Example |
|-------|----------|---------|
| API Gateway | Global exception filter, standardized error responses | Rate limit exceeded → 429 with retry-after header |
| Service Layer | Domain exceptions with error codes | InsufficientFundsError, ListingExpiredError |
| Database | Transaction rollback on failure | Trade atomicity guarantee |
| WebSocket | Graceful degradation to HTTP polling | Connection lost → exponential backoff → polling fallback |
| Frontend | Error boundaries per section, retry mechanisms | Dashboard section fails → error indicator + retry button |
| 3D/WebGL | Feature detection with static fallbacks | No WebGL → static image |

### Error Response Format

```typescript
// packages/shared/src/types/errors.ts
interface ApiError {
  statusCode: number;
  error: string;        // Machine-readable error code
  message: string;      // Human-readable message
  details?: Record<string, unknown>; // Additional context
  timestamp: string;
  path: string;
}

// Error codes
enum ErrorCode {
  // Auth
  INVALID_CREDENTIALS = 'AUTH_001',
  ACCOUNT_LOCKED = 'AUTH_002',
  TOKEN_EXPIRED = 'AUTH_003',
  INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS = 'AUTH_004',
  CAPTCHA_FAILED = 'AUTH_005',
  EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED = 'AUTH_006',

  // Marketplace
  INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS = 'MARKET_001',
  BID_TOO_LOW = 'MARKET_002',
  LISTING_EXPIRED = 'MARKET_003',
  LISTING_NOT_FOUND = 'MARKET_004',
  INVALID_QUANTITY = 'MARKET_005',

  // Guild
  PERMISSION_DENIED = 'GUILD_001',
  GUILD_NOT_FOUND = 'GUILD_002',
  MEMBER_NOT_FOUND = 'GUILD_003',

  // General
  RATE_LIMITED = 'GENERAL_001',
  VALIDATION_FAILED = 'GENERAL_002',
  INTERNAL_ERROR = 'GENERAL_003',
  SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE = 'GENERAL_004',
}
```

### Frontend Error Boundaries

```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx
// Each dashboard section wrapped independently
// On error: renders error indicator with retry button
// Other sections continue functioning (Requirement 10.8)

// WebGL fallback chain:
// 1. Attempt WebGL 2.0 → full quality 3D scene
// 2. Detect WebGL 1.0 only → reduced quality (no post-processing)
// 3. No WebGL support → static fallback image
// Detection happens within 1 second (Requirement 14.7)
```

### WebSocket Error Recovery

```typescript
// Connection lifecycle:
// 1. Initial connection attempt (5s timeout)
// 2. On disconnect: exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s, 30s, 30s, 30s, 30s)
// 3. After 10 failed attempts: switch to HTTP polling (30s interval)
// 4. Display degraded-mode indicator to user
// 5. Continue attempting WebSocket reconnection in background
```

### Database Transaction Error Handling

```typescript
// Marketplace trade operations use Prisma interactive transactions
// On any failure within transaction:
// 1. Prisma automatically rolls back all changes
// 2. Items remain in seller's inventory
// 3. Currency remains in buyer's account
// 4. Error returned to client with specific reason
// 5. No partial state is ever committed
```

## Testing Strategy

### Testing Approach

The project uses a dual testing strategy combining unit/example-based tests with property-based tests for comprehensive coverage.

### Test Framework Stack

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| Vitest | Unit tests, integration tests, test runner |
| fast-check | Property-based testing library |
| Testing Library | React component testing |
| Playwright | E2E tests, visual regression |
| Supertest | API endpoint testing |
| MSW (Mock Service Worker) | API mocking for frontend tests |

### Property-Based Testing Configuration

- **Library**: fast-check (JavaScript/TypeScript)
- **Minimum iterations**: 100 per property test
- **Tag format**: `Feature: perfect-world-neo-brutalism, Property {N}: {title}`

Each correctness property maps to a single property-based test:

```typescript
// Example: Property 19 - Password Validation
import fc from 'fast-check';
import { validatePassword } from '@pw/shared/validation/auth';

describe('Feature: perfect-world-neo-brutalism, Property 19: Password Validation', () => {
  it('accepts all valid passwords meeting all criteria', () => {
    const validPassword = fc.string({ minLength: 8, maxLength: 128 })
      .filter(s => /[A-Z]/.test(s) && /[a-z]/.test(s) && /[0-9]/.test(s) && /[^A-Za-z0-9]/.test(s));

    fc.assert(
      fc.property(validPassword, (password) => {
        expect(validatePassword(password).success).toBe(true);
      }),
      { numRuns: 100 }
    );
  });

  it('rejects all strings missing at least one criterion', () => {
    const tooShort = fc.string({ maxLength: 7 });
    fc.assert(
      fc.property(tooShort, (password) => {
        expect(validatePassword(password).success).toBe(false);
      }),
      { numRuns: 100 }
    );
  });
});
```

### Unit Test Coverage Areas

- **Design system tokens**: Verify exact color values, border widths, font sizes per theme
- **Component rendering**: Verify required fields present in rendered output
- **Empty states**: Dashboard sections with no data show guidance
- **Error states**: Failed sections show error + retry, other sections unaffected
- **Navigation**: All links present, keyboard-focusable, active state
- **Responsive**: Hamburger menu at <768px, full nav at >=768px
- **WebGL fallback**: Static images when WebGL unavailable

### Integration Test Coverage Areas

- **Auth flows**: Registration → verification → login → token refresh → logout
- **Marketplace**: Listing creation → purchase → fee deduction → inventory transfer
- **WebSocket**: Connection → subscription → event delivery → reconnection
- **Rankings**: Score update → cache invalidation → display update
- **Guild management**: Create → invite → promote → territory capture
- **Admin actions**: Warning → ban → audit log entry

### E2E Test Coverage (Playwright)

- Homepage load with 3D scene or fallback
- Server status widget realtime updates
- Marketplace browse → purchase flow
- Player dashboard navigation between sections
- Admin panel RBAC (different roles see different options)
- Theme switching persistence across page loads
- Mobile responsive navigation

### Performance Testing

- Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions (target: 80 desktop, 70 mobile)
- 3D scene frame rate monitoring (target: 60fps WebGL 2.0, 24fps WebGL 1.0)
- WebSocket message delivery latency
- Database query performance for analytics (< 2s for 30-day range)
- Docker Compose startup time (< 60s)

</content>
</file>

import {
  Injectable,
  NotFoundException,
  BadRequestException,
} from '@nestjs/common';
import { DatabaseService } from '../database/database.service';
import {
  CreateListingDto,
  CreateAuctionDto,
  PlaceBidDto,
  PurchaseDto,
  SearchListingsDto,
} from './dto';
import { MARKETPLACE } from '@perfect-world/shared';
import type { PaginatedResult, PricePoint } from '@perfect-world/shared';

const DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 20;
const LISTING_EXPIRATION_RETURN_MS = 10_000; // 10 seconds

@Injectable()
export class MarketplaceService {
  private transactionFeePercent: number = 5; // Default 5%, configurable 0.1%-25%

  constructor(private readonly db: DatabaseService) {}

  /**
   * Set the transaction fee percentage (0.1% - 25%)
   */
  setTransactionFeePercent(percent: number): void {
    if (percent < MARKETPLACE.TRANSACTION_FEE_MIN_PERCENT || percent > MARKETPLACE.TRANSACTION_FEE_MAX_PERCENT) {
      throw new BadRequestException(
        `Transaction fee must be between ${MARKETPLACE.TRANSACTION_FEE_MIN_PERCENT}% and ${MARKETPLACE.TRANSACTION_FEE_MAX_PERCENT}%`,
      );
    }
    this.transactionFeePercent = percent;
  }

  /**
   * Get the current transaction fee percentage
   */
  getTransactionFeePercent(): number {
    return this.transactionFeePercent;
  }

  /**
   * Compute the transaction fee for a given sale amount
   */
  computeTransactionFee(amount: number): number {
    return Math.round(amount * (this.transactionFeePercent / 100) * 100) / 100;
  }

  /**
   * Create a fixed-price listing
   */
  async createListing(dto: CreateListingDto): Promise<any> {
    // Verify the seller character exists
    const seller = await this.db.character.findUnique({
      where: { id: dto.sellerId },
    });

    if (!seller) {
      throw new NotFoundException('Seller character not found');
    }

    // Verify the item exists in seller's inventory with sufficient quantity
    const inventoryItem = await this.db.inventoryItem.findFirst({
      where: {
        characterId: dto.sellerId,
        itemId: dto.itemId,
        quantity: { gte: dto.quantity },
      },
    });

    if (!inventoryItem) {
      throw new BadRequestException(
        'Item not found in inventory or insufficient quantity',
      );
    }

    const expiresAt = new Date();
    expiresAt.setHours(expiresAt.getHours() + dto.duration);

    // Use interactive transaction to atomically remove from inventory and create listing
    const listing = await this.db.$transaction(async (tx) => {
      // Reduce inventory quantity or remove item
      if (inventoryItem.quantity === dto.quantity) {
        await tx.inventoryItem.delete({ where: { id: inventoryItem.id } });
      } else {
        await tx.inventoryItem.update({
          where: { id: inventoryItem.id },
          data: { quantity: inventoryItem.quantity - dto.quantity },
        });
      }

      // Create the listing
      return tx.marketplaceListing.create({
        data: {
          sellerId: dto.sellerId,
          itemId: dto.itemId,
          quantity: dto.quantity,
          price: dto.price,
          listingType: 'FIXED_PRICE',
          status: 'ACTIVE',
          expiresAt,
        },
        include: {
          item: true,
          seller: { select: { id: true, name: true } },
        },
      });
    });

    return listing;
  }

  /**
   * Create an auction-style listing
   */
  async createAuction(dto: CreateAuctionDto): Promise<any> {
    // Verify the seller character exists
    const seller = await this.db.character.findUnique({
      where: { id: dto.sellerId },
    });

    if (!seller) {
      throw new NotFoundException('Seller character not found');
    }

    // Verify the item exists in seller's inventory with sufficient quantity
    const inventoryItem = await this.db.inventoryItem.findFirst({
      where: {
        characterId: dto.sellerId,
        itemId: dto.itemId,
        quantity: { gte: dto.quantity },
      },
    });

    if (!inventoryItem) {
      throw new BadRequestException(
        'Item not found in inventory or insufficient quantity',
      );
    }

    const expiresAt = new Date();
    expiresAt.setHours(expiresAt.getHours() + dto.duration);

    // Use interactive transaction to atomically remove from inventory and create auction
    const listing = await this.db.$transaction(async (tx) => {
      // Reduce inventory quantity or remove item
      if (inventoryItem.quantity === dto.quantity) {
        await tx.inventoryItem.delete({ where: { id: inventoryItem.id } });
      } else {
        await tx.inventoryItem.update({
          where: { id: inventoryItem.id },
          data: { quantity: inventoryItem.quantity - dto.quantity },
        });
      }

      // Create the auction listing
      return tx.marketplaceListing.create({
        data: {
          sellerId: dto.sellerId,
          itemId: dto.itemId,
          quantity: dto.quantity,
          price: dto.startingBid, // price field stores the starting bid for auctions
          listingType: 'AUCTION',
          status: 'ACTIVE',
          startingBid: dto.startingBid,
          currentBid: null,
          minIncrement: dto.minIncrement,
          expiresAt,
        },
        include: {
          item: true,
          seller: { select: { id: true, name: true } },
        },
      });
    });

    return listing;
  }

  /**
   * Place a bid on an auction listing.
   * Bid must exceed currentBid + minIncrement (or startingBid if no bids yet).
   */
  async placeBid(dto: PlaceBidDto): Promise<any> {
    const result = await this.db.$transaction(async (tx) => {
      // Get the listing with a lock (interactive transaction provides isolation)
      const listing = await tx.marketplaceListing.findUnique({
        where: { id: dto.listingId },
      });

      if (!listing) {
        throw new NotFoundException('Listing not found');
      }

      if (listing.listingType !== 'AUCTION') {
        throw new BadRequestException('This listing is not an auction');
      }

      if (listing.status !== 'ACTIVE') {
        throw new BadRequestException('This auction is no longer active');
      }

      if (new Date() >= listing.expiresAt) {
        throw new BadRequestException('This auction has expired');
      }

      if (dto.bidderId === listing.sellerId) {
        throw new BadRequestException('Cannot bid on your own auction');
      }

      // Validate bid amount
      const currentBid = listing.currentBid ? Number(listing.currentBid) : null;
      const minIncrement = Number(listing.minIncrement);
      const startingBid = Number(listing.startingBid);

      const minimumBid = currentBid !== null
        ? currentBid + minIncrement
        : startingBid;

      if (dto.amount < minimumBid) {
        throw new BadRequestException(
          `Bid must be at least ${minimumBid.toFixed(2)}. Current bid: ${currentBid !== null ? currentBid.toFixed(2) : 'none'}, minimum increment: ${minIncrement.toFixed(2)}`,
        );
      }

      // Verify bidder has sufficient funds
      const bidder = await tx.character.findUnique({
        where: { id: dto.bidderId },
      });

      if (!bidder) {
        throw new NotFoundException('Bidder character not found');
      }

      if (Number(bidder.currency) < dto.amount) {
        throw new BadRequestException(
          `Insufficient funds. Available: ${Number(bidder.currency).toFixed(2)}, required: ${dto.amount.toFixed(2)}`,
        );
      }

      const previousHighestBidderId = listing.highestBidderId;

      // Update the listing with new bid
      await tx.marketplaceListing.update({
        where: { id: dto.listingId },
        data: {
          currentBid: dto.amount,
          highestBidderId: dto.bidderId,
        },
      });

      // Record the bid
      await tx.auctionBid.create({
        data: {
          listingId: dto.listingId,
          bidderId: dto.bidderId,
          amount: dto.amount,
        },
      });

      return {
        success: true,
        newCurrentBid: dto.amount,
        previousHighestBidderId,
        message: 'Bid placed successfully',
      };
    });

    return result;
  }

  /**
   * Purchase a fixed-price listing.
   * Validates sufficient funds and uses interactive transaction for atomicity.
   */
  async purchase(dto: PurchaseDto): Promise<any> {
    const result = await this.db.$transaction(async (tx) => {
      // Get the listing
      const listing = await tx.marketplaceListing.findUnique({
        where: { id: dto.listingId },
        include: { item: true },
      });

      if (!listing) {
        throw new NotFoundException('Listing not found');
      }

      if (listing.listingType !== 'FIXED_PRICE') {
        throw new BadRequestException('This listing is an auction, use placeBid instead');
      }

      if (listing.status !== 'ACTIVE') {
        throw new BadRequestException('This listing is no longer active');
      }

      if (new Date() >= listing.expiresAt) {
        throw new BadRequestException('This listing has expired');
      }

      if (dto.buyerId === listing.sellerId) {
        throw new BadRequestException('Cannot purchase your own listing');
      }

      if (dto.quantity > listing.quantity) {
        throw new BadRequestException(
          `Insufficient quantity available. Listed: ${listing.quantity}, requested: ${dto.quantity}`,
        );
      }

      const totalCost = Number(listing.price) * dto.quantity;
      const fee = this.computeTransactionFee(totalCost);
      const sellerProceeds = totalCost - fee;

      // Verify buyer has sufficient funds
      const buyer = await tx.character.findUnique({
        where: { id: dto.buyerId },
      });

      if (!buyer) {
        throw new NotFoundException('Buyer character not found');
      }

      if (Number(buyer.currency) < totalCost) {
        throw new BadRequestException(
          `Insufficient funds. Available: ${Number(buyer.currency).toFixed(2)}, required: ${totalCost.toFixed(2)}`,
        );
      }

      // Deduct funds from buyer
      await tx.character.update({
        where: { id: dto.buyerId },
        data: { currency: { decrement: totalCost } },
      });

      // Credit seller (minus fee)
      await tx.character.update({
        where: { id: listing.sellerId },
        data: { currency: { increment: sellerProceeds } },
      });

      // Transfer item to buyer's inventory
      const existingInventory = await tx.inventoryItem.findFirst({
        where: {
          characterId: dto.buyerId,
          itemId: listing.itemId,
        },
      });

      if (existingInventory) {
        await tx.inventoryItem.update({
          where: { id: existingInventory.id },
          data: { quantity: existingInventory.quantity + dto.quantity },
        });
      } else {
        await tx.inventoryItem.create({
          data: {
            characterId: dto.buyerId,
            itemId: listing.itemId,
            quantity: dto.quantity,
          },
        });
      }

      // Update listing status
      if (dto.quantity === listing.quantity) {
        await tx.marketplaceListing.update({
          where: { id: dto.listingId },
          data: {
            status: 'SOLD',
            buyerId: dto.buyerId,
            completedAt: new Date(),
          },
        });
      } else {
        // Partial purchase - reduce quantity
        await tx.marketplaceListing.update({
          where: { id: dto.listingId },
          data: {
            quantity: listing.quantity - dto.quantity,
          },
        });
      }

      // Record price history
      await tx.priceHistory.create({
        data: {
          itemId: listing.itemId,
          price: listing.price,
          quantity: dto.quantity,
        },
      });

      return {
        success: true,
        totalCost,
        fee,
        sellerProceeds,
        message: 'Purchase completed successfully',
      };
    });

    return result;
  }

  /**
   * Get price history for an item.
   * Only returns data if there are 5+ sales within the 90-day window.
   */
  async getPriceHistory(itemId: string): Promise<PricePoint[] | null> {
    const ninetyDaysAgo = new Date();
    ninetyDaysAgo.setDate(ninetyDaysAgo.getDate() - MARKETPLACE.PRICE_HISTORY_DAYS);

    const history = await this.db.priceHistory.findMany({
      where: {
        itemId,
        soldAt: { gte: ninetyDaysAgo },
      },
      orderBy: { soldAt: 'desc' },
    });

    // Only display if 5+ sales exist
    if (history.length < MARKETPLACE.PRICE_HISTORY_MIN_SALES) {
      return null;
    }

    return history.map((h) => ({
      price: Number(h.price),
      quantity: h.quantity,
      soldAt: h.soldAt.toISOString(),
    }));
  }

  /**
   * Process expired listings - return items to seller within 10 seconds.
   * This would typically be called by a scheduled job/cron.
   */
  async processExpiredListings(): Promise<number> {
    const now = new Date();

    const expiredListings = await this.db.marketplaceListing.findMany({
      where: {
        status: 'ACTIVE',
        expiresAt: { lte: now },
      },
    });

    let processedCount = 0;

    for (const listing of expiredListings) {
      await this.db.$transaction(async (tx) => {
        // Mark as expired
        await tx.marketplaceListing.update({
          where: { id: listing.id },
          data: { status: 'EXPIRED' },
        });

        // Return items to seller's inventory
        const existingInventory = await tx.inventoryItem.findFirst({
          where: {
            characterId: listing.sellerId,
            itemId: listing.itemId,
          },
        });

        if (existingInventory) {
          await tx.inventoryItem.update({
            where: { id: existingInventory.id },
            data: { quantity: existingInventory.quantity + listing.quantity },
          });
        } else {
          await tx.inventoryItem.create({
            data: {
              characterId: listing.sellerId,
              itemId: listing.itemId,
              quantity: listing.quantity,
            },
          });
        }
      });

      processedCount++;
    }

    return processedCount;
  }

  /**
   * Search and filter marketplace listings with pagination
   */
  async searchListings(dto: SearchListingsDto): Promise<PaginatedResult<any>> {
    const page = dto.page ?? 1;
    const pageSize = dto.pageSize ?? DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE;
    const skip = (page - 1) * pageSize;

    const where: any = {
      status: dto.status ?? 'ACTIVE',
    };

    if (dto.listingType) {
      where.listingType = dto.listingType;
    }

    if (dto.search) {
      where.item = {
        name: { contains: dto.search, mode: 'insensitive' },
      };
    }

    if (dto.itemType || dto.rarity) {
      where.item = {
        ...where.item,
        ...(dto.itemType && { type: dto.itemType }),
        ...(dto.rarity && { rarity: dto.rarity }),
      };
    }

    const [listings, total] = await Promise.all([
      this.db.marketplaceListing.findMany({
        where,
        include: {
          item: true,
          seller: { select: { id: true, name: true } },
          bids: {
            orderBy: { amount: 'desc' },
            take: 1,
          },
        },
        skip,
        take: pageSize,
        orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
      }),
      this.db.marketplaceListing.count({ where }),
    ]);

    return {
      data: listings,
      total,
      page,
      pageSize,
      totalPages: Math.ceil(total / pageSize),
    };
  }

  /**
   * Get a single listing by ID
   */
  async getListing(listingId: string): Promise<any> {
    const listing = await this.db.marketplaceListing.findUnique({
      where: { id: listingId },
      include: {
        item: true,
        seller: { select: { id: true, name: true } },
        bids: {
          orderBy: { amount: 'desc' },
          take: 10,
        },
      },
    });

    if (!listing) {
      throw new NotFoundException('Listing not found');
    }

    return listing;
  }
}

import {
  Controller,
  Get,
  Post,
  Body,
  Param,
  Query,
  UseGuards,
  HttpCode,
  HttpStatus,
  Put,
} from '@nestjs/common';
import { MarketplaceService } from './marketplace.service';
import {
  CreateListingDto,
  CreateAuctionDto,
  PlaceBidDto,
  PurchaseDto,
  SearchListingsDto,
} from './dto';
import { JwtAuthGuard } from '../common/guards/jwt-auth.guard';
import { Public } from '../common/decorators/public.decorator';

@Controller('marketplace')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
export class MarketplaceController {
  constructor(private readonly marketplaceService: MarketplaceService) {}

  /**
   * POST /api/marketplace/listings
   * Create a fixed-price listing
   */
  @Post('listings')
  @HttpCode(HttpStatus.CREATED)
  async createListing(@Body() dto: CreateListingDto) {
    return this.marketplaceService.createListing(dto);
  }

  /**
   * POST /api/marketplace/auctions
   * Create an auction-style listing
   */
  @Post('auctions')
  @HttpCode(HttpStatus.CREATED)
  async createAuction(@Body() dto: CreateAuctionDto) {
    return this.marketplaceService.createAuction(dto);
  }

  /**
   * POST /api/marketplace/bids
   * Place a bid on an auction
   */
  @Post('bids')
  @HttpCode(HttpStatus.OK)
  async placeBid(@Body() dto: PlaceBidDto) {
    return this.marketplaceService.placeBid(dto);
  }

  /**
   * POST /api/marketplace/purchase
   * Purchase a fixed-price listing
   */
  @Post('purchase')
  @HttpCode(HttpStatus.OK)
  async purchase(@Body() dto: PurchaseDto) {
    return this.marketplaceService.purchase(dto);
  }

  /**
   * GET /api/marketplace/listings
   * Search and filter marketplace listings (public)
   */
  @Get('listings')
  @Public()
  async searchListings(@Query() dto: SearchListingsDto) {
    return this.marketplaceService.searchListings(dto);
  }

  /**
   * GET /api/marketplace/listings/:id
   * Get a single listing by ID (public)
   */
  @Get('listings/:id')
  @Public()
  async getListing(@Param('id') id: string) {
    return this.marketplaceService.getListing(id);
  }

  /**
   * GET /api/marketplace/price-history/:itemId
   * Get price history for an item (public, returns null if < 5 sales)
   */
  @Get('price-history/:itemId')
  @Public()
  async getPriceHistory(@Param('itemId') itemId: string) {
    const history = await this.marketplaceService.getPriceHistory(itemId);
    return { data: history };
  }

  /**
   * POST /api/marketplace/process-expired
   * Process expired listings (admin/cron endpoint)
   */
  @Post('process-expired')
  @HttpCode(HttpStatus.OK)
  async processExpiredListings() {
    const count = await this.marketplaceService.processExpiredListings();
    return { processed: count };
  }

  /**
   * PUT /api/marketplace/config/fee
   * Set the transaction fee percentage (admin endpoint)
   */
  @Put('config/fee')
  @HttpCode(HttpStatus.OK)
  async setTransactionFee(@Body() body: { percent: number }) {
    this.marketplaceService.setTransactionFeePercent(body.percent);
    return { percent: this.marketplaceService.getTransactionFeePercent() };
  }

  /**
   * GET /api/marketplace/config/fee
   * Get the current transaction fee percentage
   */
  @Get('config/fee')
  @Public()
  async getTransactionFee() {
    return { percent: this.marketplaceService.getTransactionFeePercent() };
  }
}

import {
  IsString,
  IsNumber,
  IsOptional,
  IsUUID,
  Min,
  Max,
  MinLength,
  MaxLength,
  IsInt,
} from 'class-validator';
import { MARKETPLACE } from '@perfect-world/shared';

export class CreateListingDto {
  @IsUUID()
  itemId!: string;

  @IsString()
  @MinLength(MARKETPLACE.NAME_MIN_LENGTH)
  @MaxLength(MARKETPLACE.NAME_MAX_LENGTH)
  name!: string;

  @IsString()
  @MaxLength(MARKETPLACE.DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH)
  @IsOptional()
  description?: string;

  @IsInt()
  @Min(MARKETPLACE.QUANTITY_MIN)
  @Max(MARKETPLACE.QUANTITY_MAX)
  quantity!: number;

  @IsNumber()
  @Min(MARKETPLACE.PRICE_MIN)
  @Max(MARKETPLACE.PRICE_MAX)
  price!: number;

  @IsInt()
  @Min(MARKETPLACE.DURATION_MIN_HOURS)
  @Max(MARKETPLACE.DURATION_MAX_HOURS)
  duration!: number;

  @IsUUID()
  sellerId!: string;
}

import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, vi } from 'vitest';
import { MarketplaceService } from './marketplace.service';
import { BadRequestException, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { MARKETPLACE } from '@perfect-world/shared';

// Mock DatabaseService
function createMockDb() {
  return {
    character: {
      findUnique: vi.fn(),
      update: vi.fn(),
    },
    inventoryItem: {
      findFirst: vi.fn(),
      delete: vi.fn(),
      update: vi.fn(),
      create: vi.fn(),
    },
    marketplaceListing: {
      create: vi.fn(),
      findUnique: vi.fn(),
      findMany: vi.fn(),
      update: vi.fn(),
      count: vi.fn(),
    },
    auctionBid: {
      create: vi.fn(),
    },
    priceHistory: {
      findMany: vi.fn(),
      create: vi.fn(),
    },
    $transaction: vi.fn(),
  };
}

describe('MarketplaceService', () => {
  let service: MarketplaceService;
  let mockDb: ReturnType<typeof createMockDb>;

  beforeEach(() => {
    mockDb = createMockDb();
    service = new MarketplaceService(mockDb as any);
  });

  describe('Transaction Fee Computation', () => {
    it('should compute fee with default 5% rate', () => {
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(100)).toBe(5);
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(200)).toBe(10);
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(50.5)).toBe(2.53);
    });

    it('should compute fee with custom rate', () => {
      service.setTransactionFeePercent(10);
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(100)).toBe(10);
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(250)).toBe(25);
    });

    it('should compute fee at minimum rate (0.1%)', () => {
      service.setTransactionFeePercent(0.1);
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(1000)).toBe(1);
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(100)).toBe(0.1);
    });

    it('should compute fee at maximum rate (25%)', () => {
      service.setTransactionFeePercent(25);
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(100)).toBe(25);
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(400)).toBe(100);
    });

    it('should round fee to 2 decimal places', () => {
      service.setTransactionFeePercent(7);
      // 7% of 33.33 = 2.3331 -> rounds to 2.33
      expect(service.computeTransactionFee(33.33)).toBe(2.33);
    });

    it('should reject fee below minimum', () => {
      expect(() => service.setTransactionFeePercent(0.05)).toThrow(BadRequestException);
    });

    it('should reject fee above maximum', () => {
      expect(() => service.setTransactionFeePercent(25.1)).toThrow(BadRequestException);
    });

    it('should accept fee at exact boundaries', () => {
      service.setTransactionFeePercent(MARKETPLACE.TRANSACTION_FEE_MIN_PERCENT);
      expect(service.getTransactionFeePercent()).toBe(0.1);

      service.setTransactionFeePercent(MARKETPLACE.TRANSACTION_FEE_MAX_PERCENT);
      expect(service.getTransactionFeePercent()).toBe(25);
    });
  });

  describe('createListing', () => {
    it('should throw NotFoundException if seller not found', async () => {
      mockDb.character.findUnique.mockResolvedValue(null);

      await expect(
        service.createListing({
          itemId: 'item-1',
          name: 'Test Item',
          quantity: 1,
          price: 100,
          duration: 24,
          sellerId: 'seller-1',
        }),
      ).rejects.toThrow(NotFoundException);
    });

    it('should throw BadRequestException if item not in inventory', async () => {
      mockDb.character.findUnique.mockResolvedValue({ id: 'seller-1' });
      mockDb.inventoryItem.findFirst.mockResolvedValue(null);

      await expect(
        service.createListing({
          itemId: 'item-1',
          name: 'Test Item',
          quantity: 1,
          price: 100,
          duration: 24,
          sellerId: 'seller-1',
        }),
      ).rejects.toThrow(BadRequestException);
    });

    it('should create listing and remove item from inventory via transaction', async () => {
      mockDb.character.findUnique.mockResolvedValue({ id: 'seller-1' });
      mockDb.inventoryItem.findFirst.mockResolvedValue({
        id: 'inv-1',
        characterId: 'seller-1',
        itemId: 'item-1',
        quantity: 5,
      });

      const mockListing = {
        id: 'listing-1',
        sellerId: 'seller-1',
        itemId: 'item-1',
        quantity: 3,
        price: 100,
        listingType: 'FIXED_PRICE',
        status: 'ACTIVE',
      };

      mockDb.$transaction.mockImplementation(async (fn: any) => {
        const tx = {
